build_identifier_note: Internal builder/title marker. The literal string StudioSecGhost is used as the title of the bounce HTML and as the search target for the ghost-window interceptor. The companion banner window class is StudioSecVNC_Banner. The folder/post name follows the binary's own self-identifier.

c2.port_format: %u (read from binary at runtime via separate config blob; observed string format: 'Target: %ls:%u')
c2.transport: raw TCP, custom binary protocol with 1-byte opcode header

persistence.mechanism: Windows Scheduled Task (XML import via schtasks.exe)
persistence.replica_slots: multiple ('Replica deploy failed slot %d', 'Replica deployed: slot %d') -- redundant copies on disk; watchdog restores any deleted slot

victim_overlay_banner.purpose: Lock-out overlay shown on the victim's visible desktop while the operator pilots the ghost browser. Disguises the operator session as a sanctioned audit so the victim does not interfere.

anchor_window.purpose: Hidden message-pump window that receives broker commands and hosts the ghost lifecycle thread.

session_lifecycle: Connect -> Handshake -> Handshake ACK -> AUTH_LOGIN with browser inventory -> command loop -> reconnect with backoff on close

subsystem_log_prefixes.INIT: Agent bootstrap: GDI+ init, Winsock2 init, AgentNetwork.Start, anchor window creation, scheduled task install, replica deployment, watchdog spawn, banner thread, Chrome lifecycle manager launch, uninstall handling, self-delete.
subsystem_log_prefixes.CHROME: Browser lifecycle: detect running browsers, patch Firefox prefs.js to disable crash recovery, write bounce HTML, launch browser with --app or -url pointing at bounce HTML, search for hidden window titled StudioSecGhost, cloak ghost, restore wrongly-cloaked legitimate windows, hot-swap browsers on operator command, close stale ghosts.
subsystem_log_prefixes.VNC: Capture: WindowCapturer initialised on ghost HWND, GetDC/CreateCompatibleDC/CreateCompatibleBitmap, GDI+ encodes to JPEG, frame counter logged, resize tracked.
subsystem_log_prefixes.NET: C2 transport: connect, handshake, AUTH_LOGIN, command dispatch (screenshot request, viewing start/stop, browser re-detect, upload-execute, uninstall, mouse, keyboard), oversized-packet guard, reconnect with backoff.

novel_hidden_browser_technique: The traditional hVNC pattern (Pandora, DarkVNC, LOBSHOT) creates a hidden desktop via CreateDesktopW + SetThreadDesktop and spawns a fresh browser process on that desktop. This sample does NOT use CreateDesktopW. Instead it launches a normal Chromium/Firefox process with command-line flags that disable crash restore bubbles and infobars, navigates that process to a file:// HTML page whose <title> is the agreed-upon marker 'StudioSecGhost', enumerates EnumWindows + GetWindowText to find a window with that title, then uses ShowWindow/SetWindowPos to cloak it. The cloak is per-window, not per-desktop. That means the victim can keep using their visible Chrome instance while the operator pilots a sibling hidden window in the same Chromium process tree, sharing cookies and session state via the shared User Data profile.
